behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between evaluate-stage logic and artifact persistence keys. A unit test of selector logic can pass while wiring still writes to `plan`. No real OS/network/LLM dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with a real in-process artifact store (same serializer/repository used by the app). Assert `grounding` is created/updated and `plan` is unchanged. Include a regression case where `plan` already exists to ensure no overwrite.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is specifically about cross-module read/write interaction: read one artifact slot and write another. Mocking storage can hide key/transaction bugs. LLM quality is not the required failure boundary for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Seed artifact store with `grounding` and optionally existing unrelated artifacts, run implement-stage end-to-end in-process, then assert output plan is written to `plan`, `grounding` remains intact, and implement input reflects grounded context.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is conditional side-effect suppression across deep-dive logic + artifact writer. A pure unit test of condition parsing may miss an accidental write in orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage with real stage wiring and artifact store, assert no write/update occurs to `plan` while expected deep-dive outputs (if any) still occur.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly an end-to-end multi-step pipeline behavior. Failures can emerge only across sequential stage interactions and state carryover. Lower-level tests can validate pieces but miss pipeline ordering/propagation regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real chained run across all three stages with persistent run state. Assert evaluate produces `grounding`, implement consumes that grounding, and final `plan` content contains grounded elements traceable to earlier output. Verify stage order and artifact lineage across the run.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Key risk is absent-artifact handling plus safe writes in shared storage. This is a module-boundary/state integrity failure mode. No mandatory real LLM or external infra needed.
behaviors.test_description: Start with run state lacking `grounding` but containing other artifacts, execute implement-stage, assert `plan` is produced with fallback path, no exceptions, and all non-target artifacts remain byte-for-byte unchanged.
